column1: Use this space to leave any additional comments about your vision for the future of transportation in your community.
Better bus scheduals

better roads for future transportation. punctuality and politeness from bus drivers and passengers. more access to vehicles (zipcars) at more locations
bike lanes are important both for the safety of riders and vehicle drivers. Accidents involving cyclists are more common and we should do everything to prevent them

Bike paths such as the boneyard greenway are great and the one from champaign to savoy too. Inter-city transport like the KRT is excellent. I would never have one to St. Joe if the bike trail did not go there. I would be great to have a safe bike route to prospect shopping, althought spreading shopping through locals areas would be even better (what will happen to the north prospec area after some of the stores close?). Bike riders running over pedestrians

Build a high speed rail line to major cities nearby. Champaign-Urbana is great, but it's basically an island. If a person could get to downtown Chicago/Indianapolis/St. Louis in roughly a hour, it would make the city much more attractive to people.
Bus Route that can take me across town directly

Buses across 2 lanes of traffic. cars block the intersection of prospect and mattis. roads are to many close for buses to get down safely. Bus limited wheelchair space. more bus service to rantoul. cant walk at ConAir. we have better bus service to danville
Can we please get a bike path from Mansfield -> Mahomet -> Champaign
Connecting to Chicago, Springfield, St Louis etc without my own vehicle
Cool survey

Could the bus that comes down Windsor and North on First, turn left on Gerty and North on Oak? Anyone who works over in the research park area has to cross four lanes of traffic and during busy times or bad weather, that's a lot.
CUUATS is doing an amazing job in our community! Thank you very much!

I think surveys like this are important, and it will be interesting seeing how transportation in the C-U area changes with all the innovation that is currently happening. I also wonder about other aspects of infrastructure such as more efficient and environmentally friendly street lighting, adding more bicycle lanes throughout the community to improve safety, providing additional charge stations for electric cars as to increase equitable access, and smart or solar powered roadways.
I wish more drivers were courteous and less impatient/mad at pedestrians and cyclists. We need a culture change.
I would like it if more bus route went straight down main artery streets, rather than winding through neighborhoods, to improve efficiency
I would like these cities to be more walkable/bikable. I wish I didn't have to take a form of transit every day to get to/from home, work, shopping, movies, etc..
I would like to see C-U strive and achieve transportation equality, where all modes of travel are safe and convenient instead of dangerous and belabored affairs like it is now.
I would love to see safer options for bicyclist and pedestrians/more bike paths, crosswalks, and more non-drivable areas. I think green street and downtown Neil would work great as walk only areas
I'd hope to see less sprawl. Cars are necessary as long as the distance to get anywhere is so far
I'd hope to see less sprawl. Cars are necessary as long as the distance to get anywhere is so far

If I had my way, I would invest in electric trains that go from town to town, eliminating a great percentage of the need for cars, at all. I would also invest in electric train In Town, with a greater frequency of service than the current CUMTD. I would Make Sure to include days the CUMTD deems unnecessary for outlying areas, like evenings and Weekends, especially Sundays. For this reason, I have been passed over for job opportunities, simply because I couldn't get to work.
I'm happy that CU has interest, foresight, and action into doing this.

Incentive programs like coupons to local restaurants / shops will help in adapting public transportation. Also, if there are more amenities in public transport like luxury Seating, free WiFi, cafe options etc, it will attract more people. Community education program on explaining why riding bus is not a social stigma.
It should be more convenient and require less pollution.
It's gonna expand
lack of conformity to past transport. no improving in the road ways. do not destroy the in place transportation system and get away from the personal human touch
Later bus routes
Less emissions, less inequality, cheap and safe

Make bus routes easier to understand
Make it easier to know what bus stop has what bus.
making it easier for for poor people to get to the doctors, schools, groceries, etc., especially if they have kids
More bike lanes
Mtd and C-carts there to provide access points between Champaign and Urbana and the smaller towns like Tolono and Mahomet

My concern is for those who can't travel with the regular MTD routes because of an intellectual or developmental disabilities. There are not enough paratransport buses for people who would need it to travel to work because they are too bust at that time. It's also a very complicated system
My priority is convenience; I do not like investing a lot of time into traveling. At the same time, I do not like the idea of technology controlling me; it should be the opposite.
Need more buses with greater frequency. Challenge getting home in the evening because of lack of service

Neighborhoods? connect north end to service areas i.e. Hospitals? Transportation should be accessible to working class/poor. I love this initiative-it's needed!

Please create space for disability advocates (maybe check with PACE) to take this survey. I know I'm interested in reducing the car controlled spaces, but it's very important that our neighborhoods are built to accommodate everyone.

Seems good to me
Self driving car

thank you for taking the time to put together this survey. I am graduating this year and leaving town, but I look forward to coming back and seeing how much things might improve!

The bike repair stations are often in disrepair. The one in Lincoln Sq had a broken pump for more than a year. also bus service seems focused on students and not so much the rest of us. people still live here and need to get around in between semesters. this is more important in winter due to weather
The busses become more effective
The busses going through downtown Champaign are a nightmare for drivers, walkers and people trying to enjoy outside entertainment. Reroute the busses around that arena.

The Efficiency of using public transportation in small cities are not good. especially in winter, more transportation options are needed. such as one drive bicycle. I do not want to buy a bike, but want to ride from time to time. Or in winter, more buses or express buses can be available
The Safety of biking w/o drivers stopping in certain stops

The urbanization of C-U makes the community more vibrant, more environmentally friendly, and more likely to someday be able to capitalize on its economic potential. Continued sprawl renders the town more dull and boring, inaccessible, and environmentally destructive. Future development planning should have continued infill development and the active discouragement/disincentivizing of sprawl and suburbanization as its very highest priorities.

The way your questions were phrased, there was no room for any disagreement at all. "How much more do you love this soup than your old soup?" leaves no room not to like soup at all, or even not to like the new soup. I am 65. When I was younger I did a lot of bike riding (it was my principal way to get around for decades) and I lived in the center of the City. As I near retirement with mobility issues I ride my bike very little. I have moved to a one story home from my upstairs apartment. I refuse to be guilted about owning and driving a car, as I drive a subcompact 2-seat vehicle, as small as cars get. Walking long distances is undo-able.
There needs to be more clean and public restrooms for people during commutes.

There needs to be more emphasis on safety for non-car users away from campus, as well as more education for drivers on why the safety of non-drivers is actually really important. I can think of a number of intersections where drivers just don't care to look for cyclists and pedestrians (I'm looking at you, Illinois and Vine).

And screw autonomous vehicles. I'd rather we focus on expanding bus service.
there needs to be safe separate spaces for cars, peds and cyclists
Three priorities: more bike lanes and parking, more bus routes with more frequent buses, and a moratorium on sprawl
Traffic congestion isn't a bad thing. We should stop focusing on it in transportation planning.
Veorides are now more expensive, and owning a bike or car is both risky and expensive.
Very little mention of transportation concerns of the elderly and the handicapped
We need better roads
We need better roads
We need to prepare for the future impacts of global warming and population growth
We need TRAINS! LIGHT RAIL TRAINS!

While maintaining centrally located infrastructure is important, please don't forget the folks who live outside of city centers who urgently need bike lanes, sidewalks, and access to public transportation.

You can take into consideration the increase in population of people who are working from home and are not required to travel for work/chores like groceries. so personal travel might decrease and commercial might increase and the future vision can align with that

you need a way for every citizen to know what public transportation is available to them and how it can save them time and money. You need more, better, safer bike routes. public transit needs to be frequent enough like in zurich. There needs to be a way for people considering ride-sharing to feel they're being paired with safe people
